column1: Alaska Maritime National Wildlife Refuge, Homer, AK
column5: On Monday mornings they do a group walk to learn how to be a good Bark Ranger.  If you are not there on Monday, you are able to speak to a ranger to get a badge.  Dogs are not allowed in the visitor center, but are allowed on all trails.  There is a metal site specific tag. Homer is very dog friendly with pup menus at restaurants.

column1: Alum Creek State Park Visitor Center, Lewis Center Road, Lewis Center, OH
column5:
**March 2025, member reports site is back up and running. Member went to the visitor center and asked, they did a oledge and gave the dog a bandana , treat, and certificate. 
**February 2025, member reports site is closed until March. 
**November 2024, members reported trouble finding rangers on Sunday, maybe due to reduced hours for the winter. Call ahead. There is a site specific bandana.

**March mod updates with address: 
National Prisoner of War Museum
760 POW Road
Andersonville, GA 31711
**January 2026, members shared pictures of tags.  Check the pictures to see. Another member mentioned there were no tasks  but you get a certificate with a paw print.  No cost. Another member mentioned they took pawprints and stamed the park logo and date. The tag had to be mailed but was fast and were told that soon they will do it on site.
**December 2025, site's FB page: Stop by the visitor center to learn how your pup can earn their custom World War I inspired B.A.R.K. Ranger dog tag and join us in protecting this important site.
Members report that the tags are not yet in stock.

**March 2026, member reported getting the tag.
**January 2026, member shared a picture of a cute metal, colored, site specific tag for purchase. The park is building a new visitor center, but there is a temporary VC/gift shop at the just past the marina on the right side after you cross the causeway.  No other bark ranger merch/program right now. 
All of the trails on the island are dog friendly as long as you obey leash laws, of course. And mind the wildlife! There are bison, pronghorn, mule deer, and a variety of small game to be aware of.

**March 2026, member reported getting the tag.
**January 2026, member shared a picture of a cute metal, colored, site specific tag for purchase. September 2025, member adds they had a sweatring in. Booklet has activities to complete, a crossword and word search. Picked up the booklet at Apostle Island National Lakeshore Trail from a Ranger. Needs to be turned in at (and can be picked up from) Ranger station in little Sand Bay. 
August 2025, member confirms info is correct. 
August 2024, member reported that they recieved Bark Ranger buttons and an info booklet at the Little Sand Bay Visitor Center.  Member hiked the "bowl" on the lakeshore trail from Meyers Beach, 4.8 miles out and back. The nearby town of Bayfield is super dog friendly and they camped at the Apostle Islands area campground.

column1: Appomattox Court House National Historical Park
column5: June 2025 moderator reports free site specific bandana and ranger signed card. April 2025, member reports program is alive and well! January 2025, group moderator confirmed via email that the park's Bark Ranger program exists. It is a new program.  Go the visitor center, take the pledge, and get a Bark Ranger bandana.
February 2026, member shared that her dog became a Bark Ranger at the Badger Springs Trail.  No other information was provided.

column1: Appomattox Court House National Historical Park
column5: June 2025 moderator reports free site specific bandana and ranger signed card. April 2025, member reports program is alive and well! January 2025, group moderator confirmed via email that the park's Bark Ranger program exists. It is a new program.  Go the visitor center, take the pledge, and get a Bark Ranger bandana.
February 2026, member shared that her dog became a Bark Ranger at the Badger Springs Trail.  No other information was provided.

July 2025, member reminds that the trails are extremely hot for dogs paws in the summer and recommends booties. 
November 2024, member reports a button in addition to the bandana. 
Co-founder Ben visited with moderator Diane in June 2024. They give you a bandana, ask you to photograph wearing it and showing Bark principles, and email for a drawing. Note that there are a couple places to park.  He parked near the Nature Center and that was the spot to get the bandana.  Seems no specific activities to do. Also, the center is only open on the weekdays.

column1: Arkansas Post National Memorial Visitor Center, Old Post Road, Gillett, AR
column5:
February 2026, trusted member reports that the site still has no official Bark Ranger program, but confirms a site specific metal tag is for sale.  The site only accepts cash and there are plenty of tags available. 
December 2025, member reports the program is still in the works, but there is still a site specific metal tag and a generic decal for purchase.
November 2024, member reports no actual program, but they have tags for sale in the gift shop. Member reports a nice paved trail out to the river.  
Member from 2022 reports: Ranger Fields was super helpful and telling us how she’s expanding their BARK program. Currently there’s a certificate and you can purchase a small non site specific tag. They are also reordering their bandanas."

February 2026, member reports that dogs are not allowed on lifeguarded section of the NPS land part of the beach, but it's a small section and there are lots of beachfront available to dogs.  Also, it sounds like "bay" beaches (sandbars?) are dog friendly as well.
September 2025, member reports no tags, but still have pamphlet.  Also reports that the park is keeping track of interested visitors, so make sure to talk to them! 
August 2025 member reports non site specific tags are out of stock. Late June 2025, member reports non site specific tags are back in stock. 
June 2025, trusted member reports tags out of stock. 
May 2025, member recieved a generic tag and certificate. 
April 2025, member reports the tag is generic and the bandana is site specific, but both were sold out.  These are ONLY in the visitor center on the MARYLAND SIDE. Dogs are not allowed even in car, on the Virginia side.  
March 2025, member reported that the site has a metal tag and bandana for purchase.  Unclear if either are site specific,  They have an activity list to complete for a certificate.

**February 2026 the park emailed us to inform that the pogram will not be year round.  It will be a monthly event from April to October. 
**November 2025 update that there is a BARK event rescheduled to NOVEMBER 30.  Meet at Park Office, free registration, donations accepted. 
**October 2025, member reports merchandise is all low in stock, but will be restocked next season. September update from the park that the BARK ranger program is not year round. We will be doing it once a month from April to October. There will be one Sunday, Sept. 14th at 10AM and then another in October (the date TBD).

column1: Bear Creek Lake Park Visitor Center
column5:
September 2025, member reports tags are out of stock until next summer.  In the meantime, they are giving out stickers.  There are two designs and one of them looks like the wooden tag. 
Mid July 2025, member reports that the tags are back in stock.  
July 2025 member reports the entrance fee is $15. They are out of tags but have a sticker. 
July 2025, member reports a site specific Bark Ranger sticker, free of charge, but NO TAGS.  There was a brief swearing in by the front desk and dogs are welcome inside the visitor center.  Come to their visitor center to earn a BARK ranger wooden tag. This is not part of the Colorado State Park System.  They have their own admission, $10 per vehicle, not covered by the state pass.  https://www.lakewood.org/Government/Departments/Community-Resources/Parks-Forestry-and-Open-Space/Park-Programs-and-Events

column1: Bicentennial Capital Mall State Park Visitor Center, Nashville, TN
column5:
October 2025, group moderator reports there is a Purrk Tag as well as Bark Ranger tags! 
September 2025, member reports that the visitor center does sporadically close on the weekend due to staffing but should be open M-F. Also, the visitor center is dog friendly, though please be considerate if it is busy. 
August 2025 trusted member reports tags for sale but no program. Unknown if this is an official program, they might only be selling tags.  It is a pretty, silver site specific tag. Member reports a visitor center downhill from the capitol building on 6th Ave under the railroad bridge.

November 2025, group moderator reports the tags are $10. 
September 2025, member reports that this is a super dog friendly site.  Dogs are allowed in the visitor center.  The member reports that site specific metal tags are on order and due to arrive in a few weeks. In the meantime they bought a nice Bark Ranger sticker for 4.99. 
September 2025, park shared a Bark Ranger HIke series of events every weekend in September at 9 AM. Check website for exact dates. Participants will recieve a free certificate. There will be tags in a few weeks. Online registration is required.

column1: Big South Fork National River and Recreation Area Bandy Creek Visitor Center
column5:
June 2025, member reports all the train cars but one at dog friendly. The train is loud and bumpy.  Rangers are nice and handed her the pampleyt. 
June 2025, member reports tags in stock. 
March 2025 member reports that dogs are allowed inside the visitor center and once they showed pitcures the ranger gave them tags.  Manys trails to walk and explore. Site specific metal badge, reported at Bandy Creek Visitor Center, 151 Stable Road Oneida, TN 37841.

Complete activities to earn a certificate.  Please knock on front door of visitor center to let them know you are there. 
August 2024 member was told by a ranger once they determine there is enough interest in the program they would get more merchandise such as tags.  Member reported completing the Sundew trail.

Complete activities to earn a certificate.  Please knock on front door of visitor center to let them know you are there. 
August 2024 member was told by a ranger once they determine there is enough interest in the program they would get more merchandise such as tags.  Member reported completing the Sundew trail.

**April 2026, member reports tags are out of stock.  Park asked for a picture on trail to be emailed and they would mail tag when they come in.
**February 2026, member reports that on the Jetty Trail you rmight want your pups wearing shoes due to the limestone surface. Also the trail is quite narrow so be mindful for dogs that might need space. Member reports they got the wooden tag after completeing a quiz. Dogs are not allowed in the visitor center, but their's was. Dogs are allowed on the islands IF you can get them there. Many boats are not dog friendly.  The one island that is not dog friendly is Boca Chita Key/
**December 2024, member reported they recieved a tag mailed from the park.  
**Feb 2024, they have 300 tags in stock and a member recieved one. There is a pledge/certificate as well.  Dogs are allowed at Elliot Key which you can access by boat (unsure if dogs are allowed on the ferry BE SURE TO CALL AND CHECK) and Convoy Point which is close to the visitor center but it was closed for maintenance repair work recently.

May 2025, member reports that program is paused and will resume.  Please call ahead! 
December 2024, moderator visited and had correspondence with a ranger in charge of overhauling the program. Ranger says they will no longer be giving out rack cards or stickers.  Instead they are creating a more extensive booklet of activities and it will go to print soon. The ranger asked for patience as they finish revamping the program. THERE ARE NO TAGS UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE.  
November 2024, member reports the Bunker Hill visitor center closes at 4 PM and has a site specific gold tag. Previous info mentioned a tag was purchased at the Boston Harbor Islands Welcome Center, near the carousel outside the Faneuil Hall. Dogs are not allowed on the ferry. September 2024 update with a post from the park itself: "to become a Bark Ranger, you and your pet take a pledge to follow rules, respect other visitors, and keep park clean.  Upon completion, you get a sticker and your furry friend gets a cool Boston National Historical Park Dog Tag. Pledges are taken and dog tags are issued on the grounds of the Bunker Hill Monument and the Charlestown Navy Yard. While pets are not allowed in the buildings a ranger will be happy to come outside to do the oath and award a dog tag." )

**March 2026 member shared a special event held March 21st.  Be sure to check the park calendar for future events. Certificates are available, and Bark items for sale in the store at the entrance, no tags yet.
**December 2025, member reports no tags, but bought a bandana.  A person associated with the park says there are certificates for a self paced hike. 
**October 2025, park shares that they have a program.  There are bandanas available for sale for $12. There are two programs available.  One is a monthly guided hike.  The other is a self guided program done at any time.  A certificate is provided.  There are other dog items found at the headquarters.

column1: Cape Cod National Seashore Salt Pond Visitor Center, Nauset Road, Eastham, MA
column5:
**March 2026 member reports that the Bark Ranger program was a pilot but is no longer active, however, they were given a Bark Ranger stamp and there were site specific tags available for sale in the gift shop.  Dogs are allowed on beaches but not trails. Coast Guard Beach is a good one.
**September 2025 trusted member reports: There's no official program/free swag but the rangers at the Salt Pond Visitors Center did still have an info card about the Bark Ranger principals. The gift shop there also had site specific metal tags for purchase. We verified everything in our plan was dog friendly with the rangers and headed out - the majority of the coast this time of year is open to dogs. The rangers were happy to talk about it but there's also an interactive map online that shows dog closures (either for seasonal crowds or seasonal bird nesting) that we found helpful. 
**September 2025, another trusted member adds that the Park Ranger at the desk seem unaware that the store was selling anything and to remember that sometimes the info might not be well known. She furher states that the bandanas were special for spring/summer 2024 and are now out.
**September 2025, another member gave a conflicting report that the site was out of Bark Ranger items and was no longer doing anything Bark Ranger.  September 2025, member reports they were able to buy tags, but that there were not many trails for dogs to hike.  June 2025, member reports tags are for sale at both visitor centers for $7. May 2025, member reports tags are out of stock. November 2024, member reports that the Bark Ranger program is off season until April 2025.  There is a site specific dog bandana for free. Tags are for purchase for 6.95. Dogs are allowed on beaches and in beach areas without life guards. I.E. can walk through a guarded section to an open area. There was a metal site specific tag in June 2024. After June 21 there is a charge for beach parking.  Member report August 2024 that the Cape Cod Lighthouse in North Truro does not have free swag but there is a tag for sale, and is dog friendly. Sept 2024 member reports out of free bandanas for the season but member was able to purchase tag. Sept 2024 member reports ranger says phasing out the program.  Bandanas may not be coming back.  October 2024 moderator report bought tags there and they had a great time.

Co-founder Ben visited in June 2024. There is a wooden site specific badge and an activity card to complete, found at the house. Note that the house closes at 4 PM. Also note that the house is not near the parking areas.  The first parking area is next to a bridge which leads to a 3 tenths of a mile uphill hike.  The bridge is currently washed out, for at least 2024 and 2025, requiring an additional half mile hike just to the other side of the bridge.  There is a smaller secondary parking lot called the hikers lot which gives access to a half mile uphill walk to the house.  There is a shuttle running from the main lot, check the website for schedule. Ben was given conflicting info if shuttle is dog friendly. One ranger said only service dogs on the shuttle, and another said any dog was welcome on the shuttle. The grounds are all dog friendly, but note there is a goat enclosure, dogs are not allowed there nor the visitor center.  There are also working cats throughout the farm.

**November 2025, parks shares: This summer we launched our B.A.R.K. Ranger program at Dead Horse Point State Park. Here you can see Bark Ranger Gus getting his badge with his family at the Visitor Center. Stop by between 9AM-5PM to get an activity card before you hit the trails with your fluffy family members. After you check off some activities bring back your card to get a badge! We welcome dogs on all our hiking trails, our campgrounds, our visitor center, and even some of the yurts!
While the program is primarily intended for dogs, the pets we see most often in our park, we have also welcomed adventure cats, lizards, the occasional bird, and even teacup pigs. Be responsible when bringing any pet into the park: keep them on a leash, and make sure you know how to keep them safe during our extreme heat and cold temperatures.

Eggs will be hidden throughout the parade grounds and filled with dog-safe, dog-gone good treats for your pups to discover. This event is a fun way to celebrate Easter, enjoy the outdoors, and give your furry friends a special holiday treat.
Not yet a B.A.R.K. Ranger? No worries, this is a perfect time to get sworn in as an official Fort Richardson B.A.R.K. Ranger!
Things You Should Know:
Meet: In front of the Interpretive Building
Bring: Your B.A.R.K. Ranger and their Easter Basket to gather eggs!
Cost: Attendance to this event is open to the public and free AFTER regular day use or camping fees. Park admission is free with a Texas State Park Pass (purchased before event).

May 2025, group mod who volunteers: Announcing the BARK Ranger Launch on Saturday, May 17th at Fort Vancouver National Historic site! See you there! Meet Ranger Ada at her table set up outside the Fort Vancouver NHS Visitor Center at 1501 E Evergreen Blvd, Vancouver, WA  98661. She will be handing out the Self Guided Bark Ranger Tour brochures and greeting guests from 11:00am -1:00pm. I personally will be there all day from opening to closing (9:00am-4:00pm) with Harley Quinn. 
Please come and bring your dogs to enjoy this great new program. All of it has been generously sponsored by our non-profit partner organization, Friends of Fort Vancouver.   They will be selling the Bark Ranger tags inside the Gift Shop for $4.95 at the Visitors Center. The Gift Shop is open Tuesday - Saturday 10:00am -3:45pm. The proceeds from the sales of the tags will help to fund programming at the Fort and we truly appreciate their help in getting this started. You are welcome to tour the grounds during operations hours, weekly the Fort is open Tuesday-Saturday 9:00am-4:00pm. Dogs are welcome in all outdoor areas on leash with the exception of inside the stockade fences of the Old Fort, the Garden next to the Old Fort and they must also stay clear of the Pearson Airfield as that is an active operating airport.  
The other 200+ acres are yours to enjoy on many paths which pass historic buildings and beautiful scenery with interpretive signage along the way. We can’t wait to see you and your dogs there!

column5:

November 2025: The Gatlinburg Trail near Sugarlands Visitor Center will be impacted by construction starting Dec 1. 
November 2025 group moderator updates that she got the last tag at the Gatlinburg welcome center. She also reports that Sugarlands and Cades Cove has tags but certificates are only found at Sugarlands and Oconoluftee Visitor Centers. 
July 2025, member reports tags were out of stock at Oconoluftee but they get shipments in every Wednesday. 
May 2025, member reports tags are back in stock at Oconaluftee. Second week of April, a member on Instagram shared that tags were available for purchase at Sugarlands.  Admin is unclear if this is still the case.  
April 2025, member reports that tags are available online at smokieslife.org.  
April 2025, member reports tags are out of stock at Oconaluftee.  They still have the certificate with paw print and other for purchase items. 
March 2025, member reports that tags are low stock and only at Oconaluftee, sold out at Sugarlands. He also states that roads still need to be checked on agter the hurricane.  
May 2024, co-founders Ben and Nicki visited and met the ranger that created the program.  She was in the Sugarlands Visitor Center. *Both the Oconluftee and Sugarlands Visitor Centers are where you want to visit.* Site specific items for purchase include: a pretty site specific metal tag, sticker, clip-on cleanup bags, collar, leash, bandana, and dog bowl.  Most of those items were also found at the visitor center at Clingman's Dome, but that area is not dog friendly.  
A member posted in 2024 of a certificate and swearing in ceremony. There are only two dog friendly trails, one each outside Sugarlands and Oconaluftee visitor centers.  Purchases go straight to the Smoky's non profit organization. The Park Rangers will do a swearing in IF THEY ARE AVAILABLE AT THE TIME.  This is the busiest park in the country and they prioritize guest qustions and support over Bark Ranger swearing ins.

March 2025, group moderator shared a post from this county Parks FB page advertising this neat experience.  "Simply register your dog (or dogs) and come pick up your passport from our headquarters! Easy! After picking up your passport, the next step is simply visiting all the listed parks with your pup by December 31st, 2025.  As you visit the various parks, periodically stop by our headquarters and show us a photo of your dog at each park to receive stamps in the corresponding section. Once all stamps are collected, show us the completed passport to collect your pups’ prizes! Your pup will then become an official GCP&T Bark Ranger, win some snazzy gear, and earn major bragging rights! Don’t let them talk too much smack at the dog park! One passport per pup!"

January 2026, group moderator shared that the Bauer Unit of this park will be hosting dog walks called Paws and Paths on Feb 15, March 22, and April 19 from 9-10:30.  There is no Bark Ranger Program here currently, and it is unknown if anything will be given out for these hike, thought there is an image of Paws and Paths sticker on the registration website.  Spots are limited and you must register at https://fs18.formsite.com/TPWD/ix4o3akeur/index

column1: Harpers Ferry National Historical Park, Shoreline Drive, Harpers Ferry, WV
column5:
December 2025, member reports tags in stock. 
June 2025, member reports all tags at 6.95.  Bandanas are $9.  They also sell leashes, collars, and collapsible bowls.  To get the certificate you need to go to the visitor center where the bus shuttle starts and the big parking area.  Dogs are NOT allowed on the shuttle and parking is very limited.  
November 2024, a member reports that the tags were in the visitor center down in the town, which also has other Bark ranger gear.  The certificates were in the ranger station in the northern end of the town and a ranger there did a swearing in.  
Another November 2024 member report, due to ongoing repair work in several lower town buildings, bark ranger bandanas and tags are currently available for purchase in the shop at the north end, instead of the book shop (there is signage to point people in the right direction).  There are plenty of dog friendly dining options. October 2024, member reports a free color certificate. The website says "Bark Ranger bandanas, tags, and other merchandise can be purchased from the park’s bookshop in Lower Town." 
September 2024 member reported 3 different site specific tags at the gift shop for only $7.  Member saw a sign that said leashed, well behaved dogs were allowed in the gift shop. 
May 2024, gift shop closed for renovations. Visitor center does have a certificate.

March 2025, visitor Center at the Fordyce Bath House will be closed on Tuesdays and Wednesdays .  Adjusted visitor center hours are 9-5 Thursday through Monday until further notice. **January 2026, member shared that there is a QR code to scan and register. You are given a list of 7 places to go to pick up Bark Ranger.  She went to the Environmental Learning Center in Vero Beach and the people there were confused and had no clue. They went to the Preserve Field Office and were given a lot of Bark Ranger stuff.  They received the Florida shaped tag, plus the round one, which is offered in a bunch of colors. **April 2025 update, all new rangers get a pledge card and a metal tag.  If you do a Bark Ambassador training you get a bandana. 
**April 2025, member states:  I am with the FL. Dept of Environmental Protection, Indian River Lagoon Aquatic Preserves. We manage 7 aquatic preserves and numerous islands within them. We started our program a few years ago and have partnered with entities and state parks in Brevard, Indian River, St. Lucie and Martin counties. Folks can register their dogs through the QR code and they have multiple locations where they can pick up their tags. We also have a BARK Ambassador program where we teach pet owners how they can help inform others about the program. BARK Ambassadors also get a bandana. Please feel free to reach out to Tirzah.Chichester@FloridaDEP.gov." 
**March 2025, member reports this prorgram.  The website shows shows a picture of a bandana and a Florida shaped Bark Ranger Tag and mentions a swearing in.

column1:
Jean Lafitte National Historic Site and Preserve Acadian Cultural Center 501 Fisher Road
Lafayette, LA 70508
column5:
**February 2026, a member reports this site has Bark Ranger as well.  Be sure to call ahead to check.
**February 2026, from a park ranger at the site: There is a rack card with a cut out certificate/membership style card with the BARK Ranger Code/promise that gets signed by a ranger. 
Activities or checklists - On the rack card, there are 4 questions owners can answer on behalf of their pet and one bonus activity for posting a picture and tagging @JeanLafitteNPS.
Ranger interactions such as swearing ins? Rangers absolutely offer swearing in! We understand if Fido isn't with you and can deputize fur parents to swear in their four-legged family at home.
Other Bark Ranger merch for purchase to support your park?  This program is still new for our park, and we do not currently have store merch. The only BARK Ranger swag currently is the rack card and the tag. However, there are activities related to this program that will occasionally or seasonally be on the schedule, such as our Courir de Mardi Gras Dog Bandana making. This program supplies the materials for participants to select and decorate their own Mardi Gras themed bandana for their fuzzy family members to also celebrate this iconic cultural holiday.
Pics of your tag/certificate/other swag
Hours or special, helpful instructions: Pets are not allowed inside the visitor centers and currently only 3 of the 6 sites in Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve offer this program. These sites are the Prairie Acadian Cultural Center in Eunice, the Wetlands Acadian Cultural Center in Thibodaux and Chalmette National Battlefield in the New Orleans area. Each site has its own hours, schedule, and programs. These details and further information can be seen on our website nps.gov/jela. 
**February 2026, trusted member reports there is some type of Mardi Gras event associated with the Bark Ranger program at Prarie Acadian.  WIll either be the weekend before or after.
**February 2026, trusted member reports that Jean Lafitte has a site specific wooden tag and certificate to be earned at three different locations at the site. Wetlands Acadian Cultural Center in Thibodaux has supplies in stock.  Hours are 11 AM to 7 PM Tuesdays, Wed-Sat 9:30-4:30. Prarie Acadian Cultural Center in Eunice has supplies in stock.  Hours are T,W,F,Sat 9:30-4:30 and Thursday 12-7. Chalmette Battlefield Visitor Center in Chalmette does not have supplies yet, expected in March.  There is an activity list at each site to earn the free tag. Pets are not allowed in the buildings, so a ranger will come out with activity card and badge.  Answer the questions on the activity card and take the oath.  The badges are all the same.

Jean Lafitte National Historic Site and Preserve Wetlands Acadian Cultural Center 314 St Mary St,
Thibodaux, LA 70301
column5:
**February 2026, from a park ranger at the site: There is a rack card with a cut out certificate/membership style card with the BARK Ranger Code/promise that gets signed by a ranger. 
Activities or checklists - On the rack card, there are 4 questions owners can answer on behalf of their pet and one bonus activity for posting a picture and tagging @JeanLafitteNPS.
Ranger interactions such as swearing ins? Rangers absolutely offer swearing in! We understand if Fido isn't with you and can deputize fur parents to swear in their four-legged family at home.
Other Bark Ranger merch for purchase to support your park?  This program is still new for our park, and we do not currently have store merch. The only BARK Ranger swag currently is the rack card and the tag. However, there are activities related to this program that will occasionally or seasonally be on the schedule, such as our Courir de Mardi Gras Dog Bandana making. This program supplies the materials for participants to select and decorate their own Mardi Gras themed bandana for their fuzzy family members to also celebrate this iconic cultural holiday.
Pics of your tag/certificate/other swag
Hours or special, helpful instructions: Pets are not allowed inside the visitor centers and currently only 3 of the 6 sites in Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve offer this program. These sites are the Prairie Acadian Cultural Center in Eunice, the Wetlands Acadian Cultural Center in Thibodaux and Chalmette National Battlefield in the New Orleans area. Each site has its own hours, schedule, and programs. These details and further information can be seen on our website nps.gov/jela. 
**February 2026, trusted member reports there is some type of Mardi Gras event associated with the Bark Ranger program at Prarie Acadian.  WIll either be the weekend before or after.
**February 2026, trusted member reports that Jean Lafitte has a site specific wooden tag and certificate to be earned at three different locations at the site. Wetlands Acadian Cultural Center in Thibodaux has supplies in stock.  Hours are 11 AM to 7 PM Tuesdays, Wed-Sat 9:30-4:30. Prarie Acadian Cultural Center in Eunice has supplies in stock.  Hours are T,W,F,Sat 9:30-4:30 and Thursday 12-7. Chalmette Battlefield Visitor Center in Chalmette does not have supplies yet, expected in March.  There is an activity list at each site to earn the free tag. Pets are not allowed in the buildings, so a ranger will come out with activity card and badge.  Answer the questions on the activity card and take the oath.  The badges are all the same.

column1: Lowell National Historical Park Visitor Center
column5:
November 2024, member reports there is 15 minutes free street parking through the meter. They have validated free parking for visitors at 350A Dutton St., ask for QR code pass at the Visitor Center. $1.50/hour parking without code. Free parking is also available at Boott Cotton Mills Museum. The walk to the Visitor Center is 10-12 minutes, located at 115 John St. Be sure to ask for educator, student, and veteran discounts. There is no official program but the Visitor Center on Market Street sells site specific tags for $5.95.  
Mill No. 5 (close to the visitor center), that has many cute shops is closing in January 2025. Only building where dogs are allowed inside. It is spread on 5 levels. There is an elevator.  Please be advised all of Northeast sites have winter timings. Check their website before visiting and call ahead.

column1: Mississippi National River Coldwater Spring 5601 Minnehaha Park Dr S, St Paul, MN 55111
column5:
There is a free metal bark ranger site specific dog tag to earn during special events. August 2024, member was told special event, once annually in the fall, so call ahead! You can also earn the badge if you get lucky and convince a ranger who knows about the program to give you a badge. From the website about the event: 
“Learn how you and your dog can keep the ecosystem at Coldwater Spring healthy. To become a BARK Ranger and receive a free dog tag, you will learn about the history of the site, go on a walking tour with national park staff, and swear in as an official BARK Ranger. It's a fun and inclusive solution to help dogs and their owners enjoy the park while keeping it safe through stewardship.This is a drop-in event. Registration is not required.”  June 2024 a member called ahead and was told tags were out of stock.

October 2025 update from Park Ranger Sarah that the tag design is done and tags will be arriving in a few weeks! They will be on sale for $5.  She adds that there will be a Bark Ranger walk sometime in May.  She goes on to add that the park is small and the office is not always manned, so be sure to give a call beforehand. September 2025, tags are on order.  Currently they have a bark ranger pamphlet made and on the back of it there is space for it to be signed by a ranger, dated and then Paw printed by their pup. They also have a pet safe ink pad here and wipes. They usually have a stock of treats for pups that come into the office. In May they do a Bark Ranger walk program and hand out doggie bags with lots of free doggie items (treats, leash, bags bandana). 
August 2025, report that they do have certificates, and there are plans to order tags.  But this is a one time a year event.  Otherwise they have treats and always like to say hi to pups in the office. Address is 1585 Trident Rd Three Forks, MT  59752, office phone number is 406-285-3610. Late May update, we understand that it was a once a year thing and unknow for next date.  Call ahead. May 2025, member reports no cost, park is super dog friendly. The dog was allowed to come into the visitor center for a swearing in.  At the EVENT they got a lot of swag at no cost.  UNSURE IF THIS IS A FULL TIME EVENT.

column1: Muskingum Area, US Army Corp of Engineers 49320 CR 497 Coshocton, OH 43812
column5:
January 2026, trusted member reports that to complete the program to earn a tag takes some time, a few hours! Three sites must be visited, they are 30 to more minutes away from each other and then a good bit away from the WIlls Dam site, so plan on a few hours to earn a tag! He also adds that some of the driving is through Amish country, so take care while driving. He futher adds the sites are gorgeous, you are supporting the Army Corps, and the tag design is super unique!
November 2025 updateL Hours are now 7 AM to 3 PM an phone numbers are now 330-800-6507 for the main and the back up is 740-829-2051. Reminder that ticks are prevalent.
October 2025 update that the Bark Ranger program is UP AND RUNNING! Drive time.
While you can pick up a brochure in person at any of the five dams or just follow this online copy, Wills Creek Dam (main hub) is the only one you can get the tag and gift bag from. All projects are roughly an hour from the main hub, so time accordingly to arrive before 4:00 pm on weekdays.
Hours of Operation: Workers will only be in the offices on weekdays from 7:00 am - 4:00 pm. The offices operate under the federal government so on holidays and weekends (unless an event is held) they will be closed. 
**Call ahead to the Wills Creek Dam office at (330) 800-6507 to make sure someone will be in the office when you get there so it’s not a wasted trip!**
To show that you and your pet have visited the locations, a picture for proof is necessary! Upon visiting Wills Creek Dam office, show your pictures that are on your phone, digital camera, etc. to the ranger or the first office. You will be given your tag and the gift bag. 
Additional info shared is that while this is an everyday weekday thing, if a large group needs a weekend, they might accommodate.  

Address: Wills Creek Dam
49320 County Road 497
Coshocton, OH 43811
Hours: 7-4 Monday to Friday
Ph.: 1-740-829-2046
        1-330-800-6507
For GPS you can also try: 
49320 Wills Valley Rd.
Coshocton, OH 43811

September 2025 member reports this program STARTING IN OCTOBER. This is not a national park site, but Army Corp of Engineer site, Lower Tuscarawas Projects.  All swag will be free.  Site will be closed on all federal holidays.
In honor of National Dog Day, we are annoucing our new B.A.R.K. ranger program in the Lower Tuscarawas Area starting offically October 1st!
🐾 Check in at the Wills Creek Dam office to pick up your brochure - Monday thru Friday 7:00 am - 4:00 pm.
🐾 Visit 3 of the 5 checkpoints, take designated pictures, and earn your B.A.R.K. tag.
🐾 Visit all 5 of the checkpioints, take designated pictures, and earn your B.A.R.K. tag and mystery gift.
🐾 Get your pets picture placed in the B.A.R.K. ranger wall of fame!

**November 2025, member reports tags for sale in the visitor center.
**October 2025 group moderator shares: Site address: 6477 S Lee Hwy, Natural Bridge, VA 24578
Phone number: (540) 291-1326
e-Mail: NaturalBridge@dcr.virginia.gov
Fees: $9 per adult (ages 13 and older) and $6 per child (ages 6-12), children under 6 admitted free. Hours: The park is open daily from 8 a.m. to dusk, weather permitting. Visitor Center - daily, 10 a.m. - 6 p.m. Trail Store - daily, 8 a.m. - dusk Base Camp exhibits area - open Fridays, Saturdays, Sunday, 10 a.m. – 4 p.m. Food service - closed for the season
Tours: self Guided 1 mile trail that passes a cave and ends at a waterfall. Picnic areas and rest stops throughout. 
Specific Park Ranger lead programs like Dark Sky Program.
Passport Stamps: Park Ranger was kind enough to stamp the last trail map with logo stamp. No date stamp found.
Additional Info.: Gift shop, food, ice cream, info desk, and restrooms are in the main building and lower gift shop down the stairs aka Trail Store.
Tickets to enter the trail to Natural Bridge can be purchased at either location.
Accessing the Natural Bridge requires navigating a stairway of 137 steps. Accommodations for accessibility can be made upon request with the contact rangers at the Visitor Center or by calling (540) 291-1326.
Pet Friendly: All pets are welcome everywhere. Multiple access to the river. Lots of trails and places you can take your pets. Lots of wooded area, take flea/tick prevention or spray. 
BR Program  - no official program. Site specific tags are sold at both gift shops for $4.99

**September 2025, member reports program still going.  Tags cost $4.99 with a 20 percent discount for completing activities.  Their dog was sworn in. 
**May 2025 member reports best time for reactive dogs is early or late, less busy. There is a pretty, sIte specific tag for purchase, similar to the other Virginia ones. The number to call to purchase a tag over the phone: 540-291-1331. Address: 6477 South Lee Hwy, Natural Bridge, VA 24578. There is no official Bark Ranger program, but there is merchandise for sale.  Dogs are allowed everywhere but the Visitor Center.  There is one paved trail to Lace Falls. The Cedar Creek Trail is relatively flat but there are 137 steps down to reach the start of the paved trail.

September 2025, site volunteer reports Newberry is out of tags for the season.  They also close for the season at the end of September for the winter and will reopen May 2026, with new tags. 
May 2025 from the volunteer who runs the program: "Newberry national Volcanic Monument has a variety of locations throughout the monument. Our largest site is Lava Lands Visitor Center located 10 miles South of Bend, Oregon. Our other VC is in the Newberry Caldera 40 minutes south of Lava Lands. 
Both sites have our Bark Ranger Pledge cards, small and large Bandanas and Bark Tag for sale. When our Forest Service volunteers swear in the dog and human they receive a eco friendly poop bag.
Lava Lands is open 9-5 seven days a week until Sept 28 snd Paulina is open 9-4 Thursday to Sunday." April 2025, member reports the visitor center is still closed for the season.  Be sure to call in advance. 
October 2024, member reports visitor center is closed for the season despite what the website says. Member reported a site specfic bandana and a site specific metal tag are available. 
August 2024, group moderator reported the Lava Lands visitor center has a cute free B.A.R.K. Card.   There is a free map which marks the trails. The site specific tag is sold for $5.50 and the site specific bandana is $12 and there are several colors to choose from. There is no formal program, checklist, or swearing in.  They just ask you to follow the principles. Note that the volcanic fields will cut paws like glass. A member reports the dogs loved the Paulina Lake HIke.

In June 2024 group co-founder Ben visited the site. There is a proper certificate and free metal tag after visitng the site.  Group Admin Kim actually facilitated the creation of this program. Be sure to get the Bark Ranger info packet she created! It is a short walk, but note that a large chunk of the trail is in full sun. There are many stops and photo ops, the ranger will swear in. Knock on the Visitor Center door to alert the rangers. There is a dog water fountain on site.

February 2026, trusted member shared that she and her Bark Rangers became the first ones to participate at this site.  They braved the cold and snow and hiked out to the tunnel on the West Bend Rail Trail, took in a creek side view, posed in front of the Almost Heaven swing, ate a lunch, and visited the fire pit near the amphitheater. Make sure to stop by the front desk at the lodge  or stop by the Nature Center (open seasonally) to grab the BARK Ranger brochure to get started. Complete the 5 activities and return to the lodge or nature center to earn your certificate and tag.
Well behaved leashed pets are allowed in the lodge. Super friendly park staff too! 

November 2025. Find this one at the Nature Center.  Adventure awaits you and your four-legged friend – and they can now become an official West FURginia State Park Ranger. Stop by one of the Nature Center to find out how your critter can earn their official certificate & FREE West Virginia Ranger dog tag. Bonus: We have set aside special cabins, cottages, campgrounds and lodge rooms so you and your family don’t have to leave your best pal behind when you stay overnight at a park or forest.
For parks with seasonal Nature Centers go to the front desk of the lodge/park office depending on the park.  Also, there are other items for sale some of which are colored differently depending on the park.

column1: Paterson Great Falls National Historic Site
column5:
August 2025 group mod reports a site specific metal tag, but no specific actvities.  They were only asked if they knew the principles. She was told there is no official visitor center, but there there is an NPS tent near the falls during business hours. 
April 2025, member reports that there are only 2 rangers that work at the whole site and they are often out on the grounds.  The challenge in getting tags and certificates is finding them.  Please use grace and patience when trying to get the tags and certificate! January 2025, member reports the address is 72 McBride Ave, Paterson, NJ, 07501.  The phone number is 973-523-0370. There is no fee and you may need to knock on the door and someone will come out to assist you. 
December 2024, member reports this is a new program.  You get a booklet with an activity checklist and earn a certificate and a metal site specific tag and there is a swearing in. Come to the orientation table or knock on the door of the ranger office to pick up a Bark Ranger Booklet for you and your pet! The ranger office is the building next to the McBride Ave Exit Parking lot, near the Alexander Hamilton statue. During nice weather there might be a ranger info table set up near the statue as well. The ranger office is closed Monday and Tuesday. You can also ask the rangers doing the morning or afternoon tours. Rangers are available Wednesday through Sunday, 9:00AM to 4:30PM.

September 2025 update from email from Park Ranger:

Is this an ongoing program or event-based?
Our B.A.R.K. Ranger program is event-based due to limited staffing and resources. We host designated B.A.R.K. Ranger Days that include guided hikes, an information table, and a short graduation ceremony. The event officially concludes after the final walk of the day, though we may have the graduation table up longer if we think there are any stragglers for our solo-option. 

Can rewards be mailed or picked up later?
At this time, rewards are only available in person during the event. If someone needs to leave early due to an emergency, we’re happy to hold their items for later pickup, but we are unable to mail rewards due to limited resources.

Location & Contact Info:
Patuxent Research Refuge - South Tract
National Wildlife Visitor Center, 10901 Scarlet Tanager Loop, Laurel, MD 20708

For B.A.R.K. Ranger inquiries, please contact:
Sasha Azeez at Sasha_Azeez@fws.gov

What if my dog doesn’t do well with other dogs?
We understand that not all dogs are comfortable in group settings. We offer a solo option for dogs who do better on their own. You’re welcome to complete the walk independently and claim your reward at the graduation table, which remains open until the final facilitated walk concludes.

Where can I find info about upcoming B.A.R.K. Ranger events?
Please check our website and Facebook page for updates on upcoming B.A.R.K. Ranger programs.

What rewards do you offer?
We have a certificate of completion and yellow bark ranger bandanas. We also plan on getting the tags eventually! 

Please let me know if you have any additional questions! We  partner with our local animal shelter for these events to raise awareness about adoptable dogs, so it would be wonderful to see even more engagement from the community. 🙂

Sincerely, 
---
Sasha Azeez 
Park Ranger 
U.S Fish and Wildlife Service - Northeast Region
Patuxent Research Refuge 
Laurel, MD 20708
​240-882-0447

column5: **September 2025, member reports 2 different color tags for sale for 5.99 and the dog was allowed in the visitor center.  No official program. Member reported 2 different site specific metal tags. Unclear on cost.

**October 2025, trusted member reports that the October event is October 25th. LAST EVENT OF THE YEAR: 
Howl-oween B.A.R.K Ranger at Picnic Grove #13 Rangers will be setup outside of the Rock Creek Park Nature Center at 10 AM and will last 2 hours. Learn about the principles of being a B.A.R.K. Ranger and earn a B.A.R.K. Ranger badge! A ranger will be present to induct the new B.A.R.K. Rangers and distribute some fun doggie giveaways! A photo op will also be available to commemorate this great achievement! Celebrate the season by dressing up your pup in a Halloween costume!
⚠️ : If your dog requires space, Rock Creek Park events are not for you. Too many dogs in small space and too much going on. If you must go to pick up the tag, leave the dog in the car with a/c running (in warm weather). Go to the tent which is located very close to the parking lot. Let the Park Ranger know you have a BARK Ranger in the car who prefers space. Pick up tag and go somewhere else to walk the dog. It’s a huge park and plenty of trails to explore. Please ask the Ranger where not to go. 
Address: 
Rock Creek Park Nature Center and Planetarium 5200 Glover Road NW, Washington, DC 20015 Near the intersection of Military Road NW and Glover Road NW
Phone number: 202-895-6070. 
Nature center hours: Sunday: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM
Monday and Tuesday:Closed
Wednesday: 10:30 AM–5:00 PM
Thursday-Saturday: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM
WINTER HOURS START END OF OCTOBER. PLEASE CALL AHEAD FOR OPEN HOURS.

The Santa Monica Mountains NRA is holding its first ever Bark Ranger event! Bring your furry friend and join us at Rancho Sierra Vista/Satwiwa on May 17th from 9 AM to 12 PM for a pawsome free event! Enjoy ranger-led hikes where you'll learn essential tips for keeping your pet safe on the trails. Celebrate your pup with a special BARK Ranger swearing-in ceremony, and enjoy fun activities, prizes, stickers, temporary tattoos, and plenty of pet-friendly swag. It’s a morning dedciated to your furry hiking buddy—don’t miss out on the fun! Please advise that all pets must be kept on a leash with a maximum length of 6 feet. Rancho Sierra Vista - Satwiwa 4121 Potrero Road Newbury Park, Ca

2025 Event date 1/18. YOU MUST REGISTER ONLINE https://reserve.tnstateparks.com/register/bark-ranger-program. There is a 20 dollar fee that gets you a bandana along with other goodies. Join Park Ranger Stephanie for the first B.A.R.K. Ranger program of 2025! We will meet at the Rec Hall, do introductions, learn about the meaning and importance of the "B.A.R.K." acronym, and then take a short walk together prior to issuing certificates and taking a B.A.R.K. Ranger pledge. There will be photo opportunities and each participant will receive a goodie bag containing the following: a B.A.R.K. Ranger collar tag, a bandana/collar combo (sized according to what is chosen when you register), three exclusive stickers of choice, and a couple of other fun surprises. We look forward to having you and your pup(s) come out to the park!

3501 Old Nashville Hwy,
Murfreesboro, TN 37129

1563 North Thompson Lane
Murfreesboro, TN 37129

(615) 893-9501
Hours:
Spring & Summer: Every day 9-5
Nov. 1 - Feb. 29: 9-4
eMail: https://www.nps.gov/stri/contacts.htm

Additional Information: A driving tour, walking trails, museum, bookstore and living history events/demonstrations are offered from the Visitor Center. Much of Fortress Rosecrans, a major Union logistics base, has been preserved in town.
**September 2025, member reports a BARK Ranger program when visited on 9/2/2025. After completing the tasks, recieved free poop bags & dispenser, and got the BARK Ranger card signed by a park ranger. Stop by their gift shop to purchase a site specific metal dog tag.
**September 2024 tags back in stock. One ranger said it's possible in November they would start an official program.  Tags are site specific, gold metal, $7 plus tax.

**December 2025, trusted member reports that they have certificates.  There is no site specific tag but there is a generic tag for sale. 
**October 2025, member reports that there is a certificate, but nothing else either free or for purchase. 
**September 2024, member reports a bandana to purchase. There is a $10 dollar entrance fee, without the parks pass. There is no shade, so be advised in warm months. There are certificates, and a non site specific tag for purchase.

column5:
**January 2026, member reports bandanas and tags are available at the Yosemite Valley Visitor Center. 
**July 2025, member reports a certificate at the Wawona welcome center.  The park was on the last certificate but they had just gotten in an order of tags. 
**June 2025, we received numerous different reports from Yosemite.  One member claimed the program was gone and there were no tags.  Another claims that they do still have tags.  Yet another member claims they were merely out of stock. At this time the online store is out of stock.  
**April 2025, member reports tags 5.99, and bandanas, 15.99, for sale at the welcome center. 
**June 2024, member reports an extensive network of paved trails, and dogs are allowed on all of them even though there is old signage that says no.  We recommend clarifying with Rangers. They have a site specific dog tag to purchase and there is also a certificate and a green bandana. The liink to the website to purchase the tag online:
https://shop.yosemite.org/products/bark-ranger-dog-tag

**March 2026, member shows a neat ZIO Bark Ranger sticker.
**November 2025, member reports that Zion only had Bark Ranger Patches and another member confirmed out of tags both in the store AND online. 
**May 2025, trusted member reports tags are in stock online. 
**April 2025 member reports that Bark Ranger tags, bandanas patches, and leashes are in stock. February 2025 member reports out of tags and was told they expect them soon. 
**April 2024, member reportes they are out of tags for purchase. There is a metal dog tag available for purchase. There is also a signed pledge/certificate. The only dog friendly trail is at the visitor center.

**March 2026 group moderator reports a bark ranger program at this site.  The tag is for purchase, and like the Vriginia tags, not the Tennessee tags.  There is an activity checklist and a certificate.

**April 2026, Maryland State Parks shared a Bark Ranger event on April 11th at 5 PM.  Pups and pawrents can take a 2 miles hike and earna Bark Ranger Certificate.  Meet at pond parking lot. Weather permitting.
